imageImpact.title: Estimated Imaging Impact
imageImpact.tooltip: Estimate of how RA/Dec guide-error asymmetry may elongate stars in the imaging scope after combining with seeing. An estimate only — real star shape also depends on optics, focus, flexure, wind, and processing.
imageImpact.rememberTooltip: Apply these to every log; otherwise they're saved per log
imageImpact.imagingScaleLinkTitle: Open the image-scale / field-of-view calculator (new tab)
imageImpact.interpGuide: The guiding error is {{axis}}-dominant — estimated elongation tends toward the {{axis}} direction on the sky (the camera-projected {{axis}} axis, not necessarily {{orient}} unless the camera is aligned that way). Guiding-only eccentricity {{ecc}}.
imageImpact.interpEqual: RA and Dec guide RMS are within 0.2″ of each other — effectively equal, so the guiding error is roughly round and adds little asymmetry. Guiding-only eccentricity {{ecc}}.
imageImpact.interpFinalPreset: Using the {{preset}} midpoint {{fwhm}}″, the estimated final eccentricity is {{ecc}} — {{rating}}.
imageImpact.interpFinalCustom: Using a custom FWHM of {{fwhm}}″, the estimated final eccentricity is {{ecc}} — {{rating}}.
imageImpact.samplingCoarser: The guide camera is coarser than the imaging camera by {{ratio}}×, so the same sky error spans more pixels on the imaging camera.
imageImpact.samplingFiner: The guide camera is finer than the imaging camera by {{ratio}}×, so the same sky error spans fewer pixels on the imaging camera.
imageImpact.samplingSame: The guide and imaging scales are essentially the same.
imageImpact.disclaimer: This is an estimate. Real star eccentricity can also be affected by variable seeing, optical aberrations, focus, differential flexure, field rotation, wind, sampling, processing, and measurement method.

calibration.deviceTooltip: Whether this calibration is for a Mount or an AO unit
calibration.stepDurTooltip: Pulse duration per calibration step (parsed from the "Calibration Step" field in the header)
calibration.orthogonalityTooltip: Deviation of the angle between the RA and Dec axes from 90°. Values within ±5° are usually fine; >10° suggests a calibration problem.
calibration.rateRatioTooltip: xRate / yRate. Far from 1 hints that the mount steps very differently in RA vs. Dec — common when Dec is near the pole.
calibration.xRateTooltip: Effective RA rate computed from net West travel divided by total pulse time
calibration.xAngleTooltip: Camera-frame angle of the RA axis (atan2 of the net West-step displacement)
calibration.wTravelTooltip: Distance from the first to the last West step, and the number of pulses spanning it
calibration.yRateTooltip: Effective Dec rate computed from net North travel divided by total pulse time
calibration.yAngleTooltip: Camera-frame angle of the Dec axis (atan2 of the net North-step displacement)
calibration.nTravelTooltip: Distance from the first to the last North step, and the number of pulses spanning it
calibration.headerTooltip: Whether this calibration was performed for the Mount or an AO unit
calibration.headerDateTooltip: Calibration start time as recorded in the log

eccentricityTooltip: Eccentricity {{value}} — elongation of the RA/Dec scatter (0 = round, 1 = a line). RA {{ra}} / Dec {{dec}}
aspectRatioTooltip: Aspect ratio {{value}} — Max/Min of the RA & Dec RMS (1.00 = round, higher = elongated). RA {{ra}} / Dec {{dec}}

pa.tooltip: Estimated polar-alignment error for this section, from the Dec drift. Altitude and Azimuth are split by hour angle; one section can't fully separate them — "!" marks a low-confidence axis.
pa.altLowConf: Altitude is low-confidence at this hour angle — near the meridian, altitude barely affects Dec drift, so this value can't be measured reliably.
pa.azLowConf: Azimuth is low-confidence at this hour angle — near ±6h, azimuth barely affects Dec drift, so this value can't be measured reliably.
pa.confTipHigh: High confidence: sections span {{spread}}h of hour angle with a low residual, so Alt and Az are well separated.
pa.confTipMedium: Medium confidence: limited hour-angle spread ({{spread}}h) or some residual; Alt/Az separation is fair.
pa.confTipLow: Low confidence: narrow hour-angle spread or a large residual — Alt/Az are weakly separated (a mid-session re-align would also cause this).
pa.confTipInsufficient: Not enough sections at different hour angles to solve Alt vs Az across the log.
pa.effectiveHaTip: Alt/Az split uses this section's mean hour angle (~{{ha}}h), advanced at the sidereal rate over the included frames.
pa.allTip: Whole-log polar alignment from a least-squares fit across {{count}} sections at different hour angles.

ga.summaryTooltip: PHD2 Guiding Assistant runs detected in this session — click to expand or collapse the recommendation panel
ga.metricsTooltip: The non-recommendation "GA Result" lines from the log: SNR, RMS, RA/Dec peaks, drift rates, backlash estimate, polar-alignment error
